overview: Modern RTS with historical civilizations from medieval to Renaissance periods. Features updated mechanics from AoE2 with asymmetric civilization design, improved visuals, and competitive multiplayer focus.
coreMechanics: Four ages with unique landmarks per civilization
Four resources: Food, Wood, Gold, Stone

terminology.Landmark: Unique building required to advance ages
terminology.Influence: Area of effect from buildings (e.g., keeps)
terminology.Springald: Siege unit effective vs other siege
terminology.Age Up: Advancing to next age via landmark

commonQuestions: Which civilization is best for beginners?
How do I age up efficiently?
What's a good opening build order?
When should I build military buildings?
Should I upgrade or make more units?
How do I defend against early aggression?
insiderTips: Don't float resources - always be spending
Scout enemy continuously - knowledge is power
Most civs want 14-16 vils on food in Dark Age
Always be producing villagers until ~60-80 (even when fighting)
Counter units are crucial - don't mass one unit type
Sacred Sites give 100 gold/sec per site - huge economic boost
Springalds are siege killers - protect your siege with them
Keep production buildings rallied to front line

progressionSystem.landmarks: Each civ has 2 options per age with different bonuses
progressionSystem.military: Unlock new units and upgrades each age

estimatedPlaytime: 20-40 minutes per match, campaign 10-15 hours per civ
